title: Adaptive Query Intelligence (search_auto)
completionNotes: Tasks 11.6.1-11.6.3 skipped because search_auto already existed from Phase 10. Task 11.6.4 (documentation) completed.
impact: Automatic search strategy optimization based on query characteristics and cost budgets

searchEfficiency.target: Automatic optimal strategy selection with budget awareness

costTracking.target: Real-time cost tracking and budget constraints

tasks.title: Create CostAwareSearchManager Class
tasks.description: Create a manager class that selects optimal search strategies based on query type and cost constraints.
tasks.skipReason: search_auto already exists from Phase 10 with QueryCostEstimator

implementation.purpose: Provide automatic search strategy selection based on query characteristics, available search layers, and cost budget constraints.
keyDecisions: Cost units are abstract (not real currency)
Strategy selection based on query analysis
Budget can limit which layers are used
implementation.existingCodeContext: HybridSearchManager exists with configurable weights. QueryAnalyzer can classify queries. This class adds cost-aware automatic configuration.

stepByStep.details: Create the file with imports and type definitions

stepByStep.action: Add necessary imports at top of file

acceptanceCriteria: selectStrategy() analyzes query and returns optimal strategy
calculateWeights() distributes weights based on available layers
search() executes strategy within budget constraints

tasks.title: Add search_auto MCP Tool
tasks.description: Add MCP tool definition and handler for automatic cost-aware search strategy selection.

implementation.purpose: Expose CostAwareSearchManager via MCP protocol so AI assistants can use automatic search strategy selection.
keyDecisions: Query is required, budget is optional
Returns strategy explanation along with results
implementation.existingCodeContext: toolDefinitions.ts has hybrid_search, analyze_query, smart_search tools. Add search_auto to complete the intelligent search suite.

stepByStep.action: Add search_auto tool definition in the Search category
Add import for CostAwareSearchManager at the top
Add case handler for search_auto in the switch statement

acceptanceCriteria: search_auto tool definition added to toolDefinitions.ts
Response includes strategy, cost breakdown, and results

tasks.title: Create Adaptive Search Unit Tests
tasks.description: Create unit tests for CostAwareSearchManager covering layer selection, cost estimation, and budget constraints.
tasks.skipReason: CostAwareSearchManager not created since search_auto already existed

keyDecisions: Test layer selection based on query type

tasks.title: Final Documentation and Cleanup

Add all new search classes to barrel export
Add normalizer classes to features export
Run full test suite to verify no regressions

All new feature classes exported from src/features/index.ts

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
CostAwareSearchManager correctly selects strategies based on query type
search_auto tool available via MCP protocol
Cost breakdown included in search responses
All new classes properly exported from barrel files
notes: This is the final sprint of Phase 11
Cost units are abstract (not real currency) - symbolic=1, lexical=2, semantic=5
Budget constraints allow AI assistants to control search resource usage
Final documentation task ensures all Phase 11 work is properly exported and documented
After this sprint, tool count increases from 61 to 66 (5 new tools: hybrid_search, analyze_query, smart_search, normalize_observations, search_auto)

CRITICAL: QueryCostEstimator already exists in src/search/QueryCostEstimator.ts from Phase 10 Sprint 4. CostAwareSearchManager should REUSE this existing class rather than duplicate cost estimation logic. Consider either extending QueryCostEstimator or composing with it.
CRITICAL: ctx.rankedSearch does not exist in ManagerContext. Sprint 11.1 must add this getter before this sprint can work.
